name: ExtensionExampleScenario_Process_Step_Number
title: R5: Sequential number of the step (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ExampleScenario.process.step.number` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ExampleScenario.process.step.number` 0..1 `string`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ExampleScenario.process.step.number` 0..1 `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.number` has a context of ExampleScenario.process.step based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`.
Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.number` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Sequential number of the step (new)
element.comment:
Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.number` has a context of ExampleScenario.process.step based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`.
Element `ExampleScenario.process.step.number` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If step numbers are simultaneous, they will be the same.  Numbers don't have to be numeric.  E.g. '2c)' is a valid step number

element.definition: The sequential number of the step, e.g. 1.2.5.
element.comment: If step numbers are simultaneous, they will be the same.  Numbers don't have to be numeric.  E.g. '2c)' is a valid step number
